summary: Read-only runtime-version trace found strong evidence that trades 241/243/244 opened under a runtime/process state that did not match the current on-disk guarded bot_core.py. The current bot process was launched at 2026-04-10 19:50:18 local time, after the problematic trades opened around 19:00 local. The current on-disk bot_core.py mtime is 2026-04-10 18:27:35 local and already contains the reject-and-continue gate. This supports stale or different running code/process state at trade-open time rather than a current on-disk logic defect.
verification_evidence: Trade DB rows confirm ids 241/243/244 opened around 2026-04-11T00:00Z to 00:13Z (~19:00-19:13 local).
Current running launcher process creation time is 2026-04-10 19:50:18 local, which is after those trades opened.
Current on-disk bot_core.py modification time is 2026-04-10 18:27:35 local and the inspected bridge gate already continues on failed checks before open_position(...).
Log excerpts around the problematic window show BRIDGE GATE PASS events for low-confidence slugs during the earlier runtime window.
Because the currently running process started after the bad opens, the present runtime cannot be the exact process that produced trades 241/243/244.
root_cause_conclusion: Most likely stale or divergent runtime code/process state at the time of the bad opens. The exact live process that opened trades 241/243/244 is no longer running, and the current on-disk bot_core.py already contains the correct reject-and-continue path, so the rebreak is better explained by prior runtime/version drift than by the present source tree.
recommended_next_fix_target: Add runtime code/version fingerprint logging at bot startup and at bridge gate/open events so future incidents can tie each live open directly to the exact source/process version.
